<observation>

  <title>Telegram Bridge Fails Due to Multiple Bot Instance Conflict</title>
  <subtitle>Bridge crashes with 409 Conflict when another bot instance polls same token</subtitle>
  <facts>

    <fact>Error 409 Conflict occurs when multiple getUpdates requests attempt to poll same bot token</fact>

  <narrative>The telegram-kiro-bridge application fails to start because another bot instance is already polling the Telegram API using the same bot token. The Telegram getUpdates API enforces a single-connection constraint per bot token - when a second instance attempts to poll, the API terminates the new connection with a 409 Conflict error. This typically happens when: (1) another instance of the bridge is still running in another terminal/process, (2) a webhook is configured for the same bot token, or (3) the bot token is being used by another application. The bridge's auto-restart mechanism on exit code 1 will continue to fail until the conflicting instance is stopped or the webhook is deleted.</narrative>
